channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: France faces Paraguay in World Cup round of 16 as Morocco end Canada's run
item.description: In the FIFA World Cup 2026 round of 16, France faced Paraguay in a high-stakes match, with France dominating possession (90% ) and completing 77/85 passes compared to Paraguay's 4/9. Coach Didier Desc...

item.title: Bam Margera Rules Out Jackass Reunion, Expresses No Desire to Reconnect with Former Cast Members
item.description: Bam Margera, a former member of the stunt group Jackass, has firmly ruled out any possibility of a reunion with his former castmates, stating he never wants to see them again. This comes after he was ...

item.title: Morocco Advances to World Cup Quarter-Finals for Second Straight Tournament
item.description: Morocco secured their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-finals by defeating co-hosts Canada 3-0 in a Round of 16 clash at Houston Stadium. The African champions, led by Azzedine Ounahi and Souf...

item.title: Milly Alcock Reflects on Fame, Pressure, and Finding Ground as Supergirl
item.description: The article explores Milly Alcock's journey as she navigates the pressures of fame while portraying the superhero role in *Supergirl*. Alcock discusses her personal struggles with self-doubt, toxic fa...

item.title: SUV Crashes into Creek in Quezon City, Driver Injured
item.description: A SUV crashed into a creek in Quezon City, resulting in the driver's injury. According to reports, the incident occurred when the vehicle lost control and plunged into the water. Authorities are inves...

item.title: Fox News Host's Comments on White Supremacist Rally Spark Controversy
item.description: A commuter sits as members of the group Patriot Front stand at the Eastern Market metro station, on the 250th anniversary of U.S. independence in Washington, D.C., U.S., July 4, 2026. REUTERS/Nathan H...

item.title: Tanzanian fans face health risks from late-night World Cup viewing habits
item.description: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup progresses to its knockout stages, Tanzanian football fans are increasingly sacrificing sleep to watch matches broadcast from North America. The time difference of eight hou...

item.title: Philippines Allocates P550 Million for Seed Storage Facilities to Ensure Crop Supply
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) has allocated P550 million to construct seed storage facilities across the Philippines to mitigate weather-related disruptions in crop production. Agriculture Secret...

item.title: South Africa's Rugby Team Faces Uncertainty as Key Players Miss Scotland Test
item.description: South Africa's rugby team faces significant uncertainty ahead of their Nations Championship clash against Scotland, with key players potentially missing the match. Coach Rassie Erasmus confirmed that ...

item.title: Alex Eala Advances to Wimbledon Fourth Round After Defeating Iga Swiatek
item.description: Philippine tennis sensation Alex Eala made history at the 2026 Wimbledon Championships by defeating defending champion Iga Swiatek, securing her place in the fourth round. The match, held on July 4, 2...

item.title: U.S. Ambassador Denies Donations Linked to Gordie Howe Bridge Delay
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Canada Pete Hoekstra has denied claims that donations from the Moroun family, owners of the Ambassador Bridge, caused delays in the opening of the new Gordie Howe Bridge. He stated ...

item.title: Tanzania Leverages Trade Fair to Boost Domestic Tourism and Prepare for Africa Cup of Nations
item.description: The Tanzania Tourist Board (TTB) is using the 50th Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair as a strategic platform to enhance domestic tourism awareness and prepare the hospitality sector for major eve...

item.title: Nigel Farage Faces Allegations of Failing to Declare Financial Support from Convicted Criminal
item.description: A report by The Sunday Times alleges that Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, failed to declare financial support from George Cottrell, a convicted criminal. Cottrell provided funding for Farage's oper...

item.title: Democratic Nations Outperform Authoritarian Regimes in World Cup Competitions
item.description: The article analyzes the performance of democratic and authoritarian nations in the FIFA World Cup, highlighting historical trends and recent data. It argues that while authoritarian regimes historica...

item.title: Historian analyzes Trump's impact on democracy and calls for leftist renewal
item.description: Historian Harvey Kaye argues that Donald Trump's attacks on democratic principles have created a pivotal moment for the political left to reclaim its progressive legacy. Writing in Zeteo, Kaye highlig...

item.title: N137bn allocated for road rehabilitation in Borno State
item.description: President Bola Tinubu launched a N137bn road rehabilitation initiative in Borno State, focusing on the Bama–Banki Road and Dikwa–Gamboru–Ngala Road. The projects aim to boost trade, agriculture, and s...

item.title: Tanzanian Fans Face Health Challenges Due to Late World Cup Matches
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has disrupted Tanzanian sleep patterns as midnight and 4 a.m. matches force fans to stay awake, leading to health concerns and productivity losses. Medical experts warn of slee...

item.title: News Corp Australia blocks crawler bots with access restrictions
item.description: The article discusses News Corp Australia's measures to manage crawler bot traffic on their websites. Readers or subscribers encountering a block message are advised to check their browser settings, d...

item.title: Job insecurity and media adaptation in Australia's political landscape
item.description: The article explores the growing crisis of job security in modern Australia, where traditional employment models are being replaced by AI-driven cost-cutting measures. It critiques the rise of right-w...

item.title: Bomb Threat Disrupts Classes at Cavite High School
item.description: A bomb threat disrupted classes at Amadeo National High School in Cavite after a student named Rick allegedly left a handwritten note threatening to kill his classmates and detonate explosives at the ...
